description: Machine-based push/pull/lower split for a half-marathon build. Week 1 is calibration: last 1-2 reps honestly hard but clean (~2 RIR), hamstring stays on ladder rung 1. Run the 30-sec hamstring gate (GREEN/AMBER/RED) before Mon and Fri lower work.

sets.exerciseDescription: Cable or plate-loaded kickback machine. Direct glute-max isolation matching the hip-extension pattern of the stride.
sets.note: ~2 RIR. Squeeze at full hip extension; control the return. Heaviest lower day — run the hamstring gate first.

sets.exerciseDescription: Leg press with feet high on the platform — shifts emphasis to glutes/hamstrings. Full but pain-free range of motion.
sets.note: Lower-comfortable load. If the daily gate is AMBER, drop to bodyweight/very light.

sets.exerciseDescription: Abduction machine. Glute-med/min — controls the knee collapse that drives runner knee pain.
sets.note: Moderate load. Pause 1 sec at the open position.

sets.exerciseDescription: Leg-curl machine: hold partial knee flexion at mid-range. Isometric — no lowering rep. Rung 1 of the hamstring ladder; advance only after 2 flare-free weeks and on a GREEN day.
sets.note: Light. Stop if any twinge. RED day: skip all direct lower work, train upper body instead.

sets.exerciseDescription: Machine or cable press. Overall delt mass, front/mid heads.
sets.note: ~2 RIR. Priority lift — lead with it while fresh. 1-2 light ramp-up sets double as the warm-up.

sets.exerciseDescription: Single-arm at the cable tower. Mid-delt — the width driver; cables keep tension through the range.
sets.note: Lighter. Lead with the elbow, no swinging.

sets.exerciseDescription: Reverse pec-deck or cable rear-delt fly. Posture and the often-undertrained rear head.
sets.note: Lighter.

sets.exerciseDescription: Machine or cable horizontal press. Balances the pulling volume; triceps assist.
sets.note: Moderate. Balance layer — one movement is enough.
Quality run owns this day — optional 5-min core only if time and energy remain. Skip without guilt.

sets.exerciseDescription: Cable anti-rotation press, half-kneeling or standing.
sets.note: 10 per side. Optional.

sets.exerciseDescription: Pulldown machine; vary grip width. Wide grip emphasizes the taper.
sets.note: ~2 RIR. Priority lift; wide-ish grip for width. Ramp-up sets double as the warm-up.

sets.exerciseDescription: Cable or machine row. Mid-back thickness, rhomboids/traps.
sets.note: ~2 RIR. Squeeze the shoulder blades.

sets.exerciseDescription: High cable with bar or rope. Lat isolation without biceps fatigue.
sets.note: Lighter.

sets.exerciseDescription: Cable or dumbbell curl. Top-up beyond what rows already provide.
sets.note: Moderate. Finisher.
Light — pump. Activation, not fatigue; kept light ahead of the Saturday long run.

sets.exerciseDescription: Bodyweight or light-loaded bridge. Hip-extension pattern without machine setup.
sets.note: Light — pump. Alternate to the kickback; pick either.
Light. Keep it easy on the legs.
Light. Same isometric as Monday; gate-dependent. If short on time keep this over everything below.

sets.exerciseDescription: Seated calf machine. The real lower-leg gap; supports the run build.
sets.note: Moderate.

sets.exerciseDescription: Cable with rope or bar. Top-up beyond what pressing already provides.
sets.note: Moderate. Optional finisher — drop first if short on time.
